subject: Chemistry

learning_objectives.statement: Represent an ionic solid with a particulate model that is consistent with Coulomb's law and the properties of the constituent ions.
The cations and anions in an ionic crystal are arranged in a systematic, periodic 3-D array that maximizes the attractive forces among cations and anions while minimizing the repulsive forces.

depth_profile: Students must understand that cations and anions in an ionic crystal are arranged in a systematic, periodic 3-D array that maximizes attractive forces and minimizes repulsive forces. They must be able to represent this with a particulate model consistent with Coulomb's law and ion properties. Exclusion Statement: Knowledge of specific crystal structures is not essential to an understanding of the learning objective and will not be assessed on the AP Exam.
assessment_notes: Assessed using Suggested Skill 4.C (Model Analysis): Explain the connection between particulate-level and macroscopic properties of a substance using models and representations. Questions may involve explaining macroscopic properties like malleability/brittleness, and why distorting an ionic crystal causes shattering based on Coulombic forces and the 3-D array structure.

spec_checklist.can_do: I can represent an ionic solid with a particulate model that is consistent with Coulomb's law and the properties of the constituent ions.
I can describe how cations and anions in an ionic crystal are arranged in a systematic, periodic 3-D array to maximize attractive forces and minimize repulsive forces.

spec_source_citation: AP Chemistry Course and Exam Description, Course Framework V.1 (2024 College Board)
